title: VD Satheesan named next Kerala chief minister as Congress ends suspense
content: After days of intense consultations, the Congress has chosen VD Satheesan as the next chief minister of Kerala. The announcement follows the Congress-led UDF's decisive victory in the state elections, ending the Left Democratic Front's second consecutive term. Satheesan emerged as the preferred candidate over KC Venugopal and Ramesh Chennithala.

title: Fadnavis reaches Vidhan Bhavan on bike amid PM Modi's fuel-saving push
content: Maharashtra CM Devendra Fadnavis rode a motorcycle to the Vidhan Bhavan, mirroring PM Modi's call to conserve fuel amid West Asian energy concerns. Minister Nitesh Rane also walked to the Mantralaya. These actions follow the announcement of austerity measures, including halving ministerial convoy vehicles and cancelling foreign trips, to save fuel and foreign exchange.

title: 'Not done yet': Gavaskar adds Virat Kohli twist to 'Gen Next' debate
content: Sunil Gavaskar lauded Virat Kohli's spectacular century, proving the enduring class of experienced players.  Kohli's masterclass in chasing, his ninth IPL hundred, and becoming the fastest to 14,000 T20 runs silenced doubters.  His innings highlighted his unparalleled ability to construct a chase, a feat few can match, reaffirming his legendary status.

title: 'Bengal not bulldozer state': What lawyer Mamata said in her 1st appearance in Calcutta HC
content: Former West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee appeared before the Calcutta High Court, urging protection for citizens amidst allegations of post-poll violence. She accused police of inaction and highlighted incidents of attacks, intimidation, and threats, seeking urgent judicial intervention to ensure people's safety.

title: Meta's highest-paid employee is not happy with 'just money motivated' tag for his team
content: Meta SuperIntelligence Lab chief Alexandr Wang is pushing back on the idea that his team joined for the money. On the Core Memory podcast, Wang said the real draw was compute access, talent density, and research freedom—not the $100 million packages Meta reportedly offered. He also addressed the viral soup story involving Mark Zuckerberg and an OpenAI recruit.

title: History-maker at 12, how Faustino Oro became 'Messi of Chess'
content: Faustino Oro, a 12-year-old Argentine chess prodigy known as the “Messi of Chess,” has become the second-youngest Grandmaster in history. TimesofIndia.com follows his journey from learning chess during the COVID lockdown in Buenos Aires to moving to Barcelona and competing globally. Despite tougher FIDE rules, Oro’s talent, discipline, and love for the game helped him succeed.

title: Hormuz crisis: India eyes Rs 40k crore subsea pipeline for uninterrupted gas supplies
content: The proposed Middle East-India Deep-water Pipeline (MEIDP) is planned as a 2,000-kilometre underwater network running beneath the Arabian Sea and connecting Oman directly with the Gujarat coastline.

title: Why Trump aides left their phones behind before entering China
content: During President Trump's visit to China, American officials and CEOs implemented a 'digital lockdown,' using 'clean' devices and leaving personal electronics behind. This precaution stems from a long-standing US government assumption that all devices and networks in China are potentially compromised and subject to surveillance.

title: Will Hardik Pandya play today against Punjab Kings in Dharamsala?
content: Mumbai Indians face Punjab Kings in Dharamsala, with captain Hardik Pandya highly unlikely to play due to back spasms. Suryakumar Yadav's availability is also uncertain as he remains in Mumbai after the birth of his child. Despite being eliminated, MI can impact Punjab's playoff hopes.

title: Why rising temperatures are triggering more snakebite incidents across India
content: As summer heat intensifies, India faces a surge in deadly snakebite incidents, with states like Karnataka and Kerala reporting significant increases. Experts attribute this rise to snakes seeking cooler temperatures and escaping monsoon floods, leading to more human encounters. Delayed treatment and reliance on myths tragically cost lives, underscoring the urgent need for immediate medical attention and preventative measures.

title: Amazon cuts more jobs after 30,000 layoffs; says it regularly reviews organisation to meet goals
content: Amazon continues its workforce reduction, now impacting its Selling Partner Services division after significant layoffs earlier this year. This move aligns with CEO Andy Jassy's focus on efficiency and cost discipline. Simultaneously, the e-commerce giant is heavily investing in AI, a strategy Jassy believes will drive substantial long-term growth and redefine customer experiences.

title: Who is VD Satheesan? Kerala’s new CM & grassroots leader who led UDF’s comeback
content: VD Satheesan has been appointed the new Kerala chief minister. The Congress party announced his selection after a period of deliberation. Satheesan, a lawyer and social worker with a strong legislative background, previously served as the leader of the opposition, guiding the party's resurgence.

title: Ahmedabad: Woman staffer caught on CCTV pocketing gold jewellery worth Rs 1.7 crore
content: A jeweller in Ahmedabad was shocked to discover an employee allegedly stole gold ornaments worth Rs 1.7 crore. CCTV footage reportedly shows the sales and marketing staffer pocketing chains, rings, and mangalsutras before leaving the showroom. Police are now actively searching for the accused woman.

title: 'It’s going to finish one day': Virat Kohli makes honest reflection
content: Virat Kohli delivered a masterful performance, smashing his ninth IPL century to lead Royal Challengers Bengaluru to a successful chase of 193 against Kolkata Knight Riders. This historic innings saw Kohli become the fastest player to 14,000 T20 runs and the first Indian with 10 T20 centuries, solidifying his legacy as a supreme chaser.

title: NEET UG paper leak case: 17-year-old Goa teen ends life over cancelled exam
content: A 17-year-old student from Curtorim died by suicide after being distressed by the NEET cancellation due to a paper leak. He left a note expressing his disinterest in further competitive exams. His father discovered the body at their residence.

title: Red Fort blast case: NIA files 7,500-page chargesheet in terror plot, names 10 accused
content: A massive 7,500-page chargesheet has been filed by the NIA against 10 individuals linked to the deadly Red Fort car bomb blast in November 2025. The accused, associated with an Al-Qaeda offshoot, allegedly plotted to overthrow the Indian government and impose Sharia rule. Investigations revealed procurement of specialized equipment and manufacturing of explosives by radicalized medical professionals.

title: NEET-UG leak: Handwritten question paper, scanned & circulated; students paid lakhs, probe finds
content: A father in Rajasthan allegedly scanned a handwritten NEET UG 2026 question paper, turning it into PDFs for aspirants. This development follows the NTA's cancellation of the exam, impacting numerous medical hopefuls.  Investigators are probing how the paper was accessed and circulated, with some students reportedly paying substantial sums for leaked material.

title: 89 killed as fierce storm and heavy rain batter multiple districts in Uttar Pradesh
content: Powerful thunderstorms, lightning, and strong winds have claimed at least 89 lives across Uttar Pradesh. The devastating storms, which lasted for hours, also injured 53 people and killed 114 animals. Widespread destruction of homes and property was reported, with collapsing walls and falling trees being the primary causes of fatalities.

title: Why gold, silver duty hike to 15% is unlikely to hit demand for precious metals
content: The move to up the gold import duty comes days after PM Narendra Modi appeals to citizens to avoid unnecessary gold purchases for a year. But is a hike in duties an effective way to curb consumption? We take a look:

title: India asks US for waiver on Russian oil as Iran war squeezes energy supply: Report
content: India has requested the US to extend its waiver on Russian oil imports due to ongoing disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz. This waiver, crucial for stabilizing oil markets, is set to expire on May 16. Despite US pressure to reduce discounted Russian oil purchases, India prioritizes stable energy supplies, warning of economic consequences from market volatility.

title: 'How you study matters more than hrs': Class XII CBSE topper's success mantra
content: Bhavya Ranjan, a humanities student from Ranchi, achieved national topper status in CBSE Class 12 with 99.8%. Her success stemmed from consistent preparation, focusing on understanding concepts rather than long study hours. Supported by her school's "Target 100" initiative and a nurturing family environment, Bhavya's achievement highlights the importance of sustained effort and a conducive atmosphere for academic excellence.

title: Mamata dons lawyers' robes, arrives at Calcutta HC in post-poll violence case
content: TMC chief and former West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee appeared at the Calcutta High Court in a lawyer's attire. She is set to question proceedings in a post-poll violence Public Interest Litigation case filed by Shirshanya Bandopadhyay, son of TMC leader Kalyan Bandopadhyay.

title: 'Bade logon ko bachaya ja raha hai': What NEET-UG leak case accused claimed after arrest
content: An accused in the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak case claimed "big people are being saved" while in CBI custody, intensifying speculation about influential players higher up the chain. The CBI has arrested five individuals and is investigating a leak network spanning Maharashtra, Haryana, and Rajasthan, with the "guess paper" allegedly circulating through various channels.

title: Price prediction: Will gold head to Rs 1.70 lakh/10 gms & silver hit Rs 3.4 lakh/kg?
content: Gold and silver price prediction today: According to Abhilash Koikkara, Head - Forex & Commodities, Nuvama Professional Clients Group, both gold and silver prices are exhibiting a bullish bias this week.

title: ‘Letter in drawer’: Trump left succession instructions for JD Vance in Oval Office
content: A senior White House official revealed President Trump has left detailed instructions for Vice President Vance in a letter at the Resolute Desk for emergencies.  These remarks surfaced during Trump's China visit, with the official downplaying assassination fears despite Trump being called an "existential threat" to China.  The article also recounts past assassination attempts on the President.

title: Woman allegedly gangraped in private bus in Delhi; driver, conductor arrested
content: Delhi Police have registered a case and arrested two individuals after a woman alleged gangrape in a private bus. The bus used in the incident has been seized, and authorities are conducting a thorough investigation into the matter from all possible angles.

title: Viral video shows children sitting in car boot as vehicle speeds past 100 kmph in Hyderabad
content: A viral video from Hyderabad's Outer Ring Road shows a family traveling at over 100 kmph with at least three children seated in the car's open boot. The dangerous act has sparked outrage online, prompting Cyberabad Traffic Police to promise necessary action as per safety regulations.

title: 4 dead, 10 injured after bus-tanker collision triggers massive fire on Gujarat highway
content: A tragic collision between a private luxury bus and an asphalt-laden tanker near Sangani village in Gujarat's Surendranagar district resulted in a devastating fire. The incident, which occurred early Thursday morning, claimed the lives of at least four passengers and left ten others with burn injuries. The bus was en route from Ahmedabad to Rajkot when the accident happened.

title: Rain threat looms large: What happens if PBKS vs MI gets washed out?
content: A looming rain threat in Dharamshala could significantly impact Punjab Kings' playoff aspirations as they face Mumbai Indians. A washout would grant both teams a point, potentially hindering PBKS's chances of securing a top-two finish and relying on split points rather than outright wins.

title: Bethell loses gold chain in RCB vs KKR match - here's what happened next
content: Jacob Bethell accidentally dropped his gold chain while getting dismissed during Royal Challengers Bengaluru’s IPL 2026 clash against Kolkata Knight Riders in Raipur. Devdutt Padikkal later picked it up and returned it to him. The match, however, belonged to Virat Kohli, whose unbeaten 105 guided RCB to a six-wicket win and the top of the points table.

title: BITS Goa reports 2nd student suicide this year; PhD scholar found dead
content: A 26-year-old PhD scholar from Kerala was found dead at a rented accommodation in Verna, marking the second student suicide at BITS Pilani's Goa Campus this year. Authorities are investigating the incident, with preliminary findings pointing to suicide. The recurring deaths are attributed to 'suicide contagion,' prompting the institution to enhance mental health support and reduce academic pressure.

title: 'Have entered the fray to abolish Sanatana': Now, TVK MLA Mustafa endorses Stalin's remark
content: TVK MLA VMS Mustafa's remarks endorsing DMK leader Udhayanidhi Stalin's call to "abolish Sanatan" have sparked a political controversy in Tamil Nadu. Mustafa stated that his party also subscribes to the ideals of Periyar and Ambedkar, aiming to eradicate Sanatana. These comments come after Udhayanidhi reignited the debate in the Assembly, drawing criticism from the BJP.

title: After hantavirus, now norovirus? How a gut bug forced a France cruise ship into lockdown
content: French authorities have eased restrictions on the cruise ship Ambition after confirming a viral gastrointestinal outbreak, not hantavirus, sickened dozens. Around 80 passengers and crew experienced symptoms, with no severe cases reported. A passenger's death was attributed to a heart attack, unrelated to the illness. The ship, carrying over 1,700 people, was temporarily restricted as a precaution.

title: Michael Jackson's daughter Paris Jackson wins $625,000 legal case
content: In a landmark ruling, Paris Jackson, the daughter of the iconic Michael Jackson, has triumphed over a controversial $625,000 bonus payout authorized by the estate's executors. A Los Angeles judge has decreed that those funds, previously given to outside law firms, must be refunded to the estate.

title: Two more India-bound ships cross Hormuz, taking total to 13
content: Two more LPG carriers, foreign-flagged Symi and NV Sunshine, successfully navigated the Strait of Hormuz, bringing the total India-bound vessels to 13. These shipments, carrying vital cooking gas and crude oil, underscore ongoing efforts to ensure energy security.  The safe passage highlights robust inter-ministerial coordination amidst regional tensions.

title: Cap, blue bag, knife: How elderly man posing as mechanic terrorised Delhi neighbourhoods
content: A 55-year-old man, Ajay Bagga, has been apprehended by Delhi Police for a series of robberies in Keshav Puram and Pitampura. Posing as a water purifier mechanic, Bagga used a knife and axe-like weapon in two violent incidents, causing panic. He was arrested after a manhunt involving CCTV analysis and technical surveillance.

title: Kerala CM announcement LIVE: AICC announces V.D. Satheesan as CM designate; accept Congress decision, says K.C. Venugopal
content: With the announcement, Congress will proceed to form the government in the State, ending days of speculation as the top leaders tussled among themselves for the post

title: Mamata appears before Calcutta High Court to argue Bengal post-poll violence case
content: According to the petitioner, several TMC leaders and workers were allegedly forced to flee their homes after the polls, while many of them were attacked “because of their association with the Trinamool Congress”

title: BRICS Foreign Ministers’ summit: Conflict in West Asia merits particular attention, says Jaishankar
content: BRICS Foreign Ministers and Heads of Delegation from Member and partner countries will participate in the meeting

title: Red Fort bomb blast case: NIA files 7,500-page charge sheet against 10 accused
content: Apart from Dr. Nabi, the others named in the charge sheet are Aamir Rashid Mir, Jasir Bilal Wani, Dr. Muzamil Shakeel, Dr. Adeel Ahmed Rather, Dr. Shaheen Saeed, Mufti Irfan Ahmad Wagay, Soyab, Dr. Bilal Naseer Malla and Yasir Ahmad Dar

title: West Bengal government directs schools to mandatorily include singing of Vande Mataram in morning assemblies
content: The directive states that every student must participate in singing the national song at the start of the school day, and heads of institutions have been instructed to ensure strict compliance

title: V.D. Satheesan to be next Chief Minister of Kerala as Congress leadership ends suspense
content: It was not immediately evident how the Congress leadership had eventually resolved the dispute among Mr. Satheesan, Ramesh Chennithala and and K.C. Venugopal, the three senior leaders who were in the running for the Chief Minister’s post

title: ST quota: Why Congress and BJP are wary of raising the pitch in Karnataka
content: Leadership tussle seems to have held back the Congress while the BJP is grappling with divided opinions and competing interests on the issue of ST quota

title: Ten new MLCs sworn in at Maharashtra Vidhan Bhavan
content: Legislative Council chairperson Ram Shinde administered the oath to the members at the Central Hall in the Vidhan Bhavan

title: Congress demands Bandi Sanjay’s resignation
content: Congress calls for Bandi Sanjay’s resignation amid POCSO case involving his son, demanding accountability and full cooperation with the investigation

title: Woman gang-raped in sleeper bus in New Delhi
content: The two accused have been arrested in connection with the incident that took place on May 12, the sources said

title: ST quota back at 3% raises concerns over  proportional representation
content: While the 1931 census put ST population at about 0.8%, with more tribes added to the list, it is now estimated to be over 7% of Karnataka’s population

title: What students in Karnataka can wear under the new uniform rules
content: While no student in Karnataka can be denied entry into an educational institution, classroom, examination hall or for any academic activity for wearing permitted symbols, in examinations, any national or State-level dress code rules already in place will continue to apply.

title: How is the anti-defection law going to operate in AIADMK’s case?
content: There are two grounds for disqualification in the event of defection — when a member voluntarily gives up membership of his or her political party and when a member votes or abstains from voting, contrary to any direction issued by his or her political party

title: ‘Limited’ religious symbols to be allowed with uniforms in Karnataka schools and colleges
content: The May 13 order revokes the previous BJP government’s February 5, 2022, directive, which had required students to wear prescribed uniforms and had become the basis for restrictions that later triggered the hijab row

title: Thoothukudi will see highest Tasmac closures of liquor shops near schools, places of worship
content: Employees working at Tasmac outlets in the Madurai region said the government must ensure adequate police deployment in these areas following the closures

title: ‘Union Government should include enhanced quota under 9th schedule’
content: Karnataka’s Public Works Minister Satish Jarkiholi, who belongs to the Nayaka community, a scheduled tribe, spoke to The Hindu

title: Panchamasali seer appears before Harihar police for questioning in POCSO case in Karnataka
content: Sri Vachanananda Swami has been accused of inappropriate behaviour with a minor boy who was staying in the free hostel run by the Veerashaiva Lingayat Panchamasali Guru Peetha in Harihar

title: India will ensure zero casualty during heat waves: Amit Shah
content: The Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) has prepared a plan to better combat the "serious challenge" of heat waves in the country and we will be able to ensure "zero casualty" in this context over the next few years, says Amit Shah

title: Nearly 90 people killed as storm and rain batter Uttar Pradesh; relief work under way
content: The Relief Commissioner’s office said all divisional commissioners and district magistrates have been directed to verify the incidents “with full sensitivity” and ensure financial assistance is distributed within 24 hours

title: NEET paper leak case: CBI arrests five, conducts searches across the country
content: Incriminating material and electronic devices seized, following leads through “extensive technical and forensic analysis”, says CBI; two more suspects detained in Maharashtra, handed over to CBI

title: Six militants arrested in Manipur
content: A 24-year-old member of the banned United National Liberation Front (Pambei) was also arrested from Lilong Chajing area in Thoubal district for his alleged involvement in a bomb explosion in the State

title: Signal fault disrupts Chennai suburban train services on Tiruvallur-Avadi line
content: A technical fault in the signalling system near the Perambur railway station, at around 5.15 a.m. on Thursday, resulted in more than three suburban trains proceeding from Arakkonam via Tiruvallur and Avadi getting stopped

title: The ‘Kutty Story’ phenomenon: How Vijay prepared voters with his audio launch speeches
content: From ‘Kutty Stories’ that captivated the youth to punchlines that advised them to aim big and live better, Vijay’s captivating speeches have been instrumental in transforming his film fandom into a robust voter base

title: India bans sugar exports till September 30
content: The notification issued by the Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT) on May 13 said this order does not apply to sugar being exported to the European Union and the U.S. under the tariff rate quota scheme

title: U.P. Assembly suspends parliamentary committees’ study tours till further orders
content: The orders issued by the Assembly Secretariat’s Principal Secretary, Pradeep Kumar Dubey, have come into immediate effect, officials said

title: Ex-Army chief Naravane backs RSS leader's remarks on dialogue with Pakistan
content: He was responding to RSS General Secretary Dattatreya Hosabale's statement regarding the desirability of engaging in dialogue

title: Kashmir MLA ‘boycotts’ L-G’s drug campaign after demolition drive in his constituency
content: “For decades, people in J&K saw drugs and terrorism as separate threats. But they are not two problems; they are the two heads of the same snake,” said L-G Sinha

title: Supreme Court directs States to install panic buttons, tracking devices in public transport vehicles
content: Fitness certificates or permits should not be issued to public service vehicles found in violation of these safety measures, apex court directs

title: Congress MLA-elects make a beeline for Indira Bhavan as AICC inches closer to announcing its Chief Minister pick for Kerala
content: Congress workers trickled into party offices in the State, as their eagerness to know the party high command’s choice for the top post appeared to reach a feverish pitch

title: 50 years of Annakili: The film that launched Ilaiyaraaja’s career
content:
Fifty years have passed like a fleeting moment, but the songs of Annakili continue to haunt generations of music lovers. With the film’s release began the extraordinary musical journey of Ilaiyaraaja, whose career would later rise to unparalleled heights.

Gangai Amaran, who worked closely with his brother during the composing sessions and served as the rhythm guitarist for the film, recalls those memorable days with B. Kolappan.

“The higher octaves in the humming of the song ‘Annakili Unnai Theduthey’ symbolically foreshadowed the great heights Ilaiyaraaja was destined to achieve,” he said.

title: Iran Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi arrives in India, to attend BRICS FM meet
content: Araghchi will be coming face to face with UAE Minister of State for Foreign Affairs on Thursday (May 14, 2026) at BRICS Foreign Ministers summit

title: Vijay Narayan earns rare distinction of being Advocate General under two different governments
content: The government led by TVK appoints him as the top most law officer of the State; he had held the office under the AIADMK government too between 2017 and 2021

title: Himanta welcomes Bengal cabinet’s decision to hand over land to BSF for border fencing
content: Mr. Sarma said that many organisations in Assam demand that the border must be sealed in the State, but this was meaningless without a similar closure in West Bengal

title: Annakili@50: The extraordinary journey of maestro Ilaiyaraaja
content: If there had been no Panchu Arunachalam, there would have been no Ilaiyaraaja or Gangai Amaran. He was our first god. He lit the light in our lives, says Gangai Amaran, younger brother of Ilaiyaraaja, who was the rhythm guitarist for the film

title: 4 killed, 10 injured as bus catches fire after colliding with tanker in Gujarat's Surendranagar
content: There were around 40 passengers in the bus, and several of them were rescued, Deputy Superintendent of Police said.

title: Karnataka’s Leader of Opposition R Ashok to reduce escort vehicles, travel by Namma Metro
content: Karnataka’s Leader of Opposition R. Ashok announced that he and his family would abide by austerity measures

title: Russia to fulfil all agreements on energy supply to India: FM Lavrov
content: Stating that 'Hindi-Rusi bhai bhai’ (Indians and Russians are brothers), Mr. Lavrov said that's not just a fun slogan to chant, it has become part of our culture

title: Rajasthan CM seeks speedy completion of projects under Yamuna water pact
content: Mr. Sharma met his Haryana counterpart Nayab Singh Saini and Union Jal Shakti Minister C.R. Patil during his visit to New Delhi and deliberated on a timeline for laying the pipelines

title: Iran working on Hormuz ‘protocol’ to cover ‘costs’, says Deputy Foreign Minister Gharibabadi
content: Hinting at UAE, Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi says “a neighbouring country that is also a member of the BRICS” is trying to scuttle a joint statement by seeking condemnation of Iran

title: Activists rue budget cut for promoting millet cultivation in Odisha
content: Right To Food campaign allege the government has reduced funding and withdrawn ground-level support agencies for millet mission

title: Congress high command defers announcement of Kerala CM designate to May 14
content: High command asks Congress MLAs-elect  to report to Indira Bhavan in Thiruvananthapuram, even as Congress workers congregate in front of the houses of Venugopal, Chennithala and Satheesan in anticipation of the momentous announcement from New Delhi

title: West Bengal CM gives sanction to CBI to prosecute officials in several scams
content: Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari announced that the policy of the previous government relating to a ban on the sale of potatoes to other states stands lifted with immediate effect

title: NSUI holds protest outside Education Minister’s residence over NEET paper leak
content: The protest march highlighted that the paper leak had “shattered the dreams of millions of students”, demanding immediate resignation of Union Minister Dharmendra Pradhan

title: In 2024, NTA and Govt. did not want to cancel NEET, cited student welfare in Supreme Court
content: Two years ago, the government had argued that cancelling the exam would be like treating “unequals equally” and that innocents and wrongdoers would be made to suffer same consequences

title: A history of rebellions within the AIADMK
content: Rebellions are not new to the AIADMK, with the party having seen similar disruptions over the years. Here’s a timeline of events that track dissent within the party since its formation in 1972

title: Nicobarese oppose proposal for three wildlife sanctuaries
content: Tribal Council says Centre did not consult residents of Little Nicobar, Meroe, and Menchal islands, where the sanctuaries are being planned

title: DMK boycotts floor test; urges CM to continue with Dravidian model welfare schemes
content: Opposition Leader Udhayanidhi Stalin asks the new government to continue the DMK government’s schemes

title: Prateek Yadav Had History Of Blood Clots, Was At High Risk: Family Doctor
content: Prateek Yadav, who was a fitness enthusiast, was unable to workout regularly because of his busy schedule, his family doctor revealed.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Congress's Old Problem Is Back: Over-Confidence Without Numbers
content: Rahul Gandhi's recent assertion at a rally in Gurugram, that the Congress alone can defeat the BJP, is puzzling. When has arrogance ever worked for the party?

title: Macron's Wife 'Slapped' Him Over Flirty Text With Iranian Actor: Report
content: The claim was made in a new book titled "An (almost) perfect couple" by Paris Match journalist Florian Tardif. In the book, Tardif alleged that Macron, 48, and Farahani, 42, were in a 'platonic relationship' for several months.

title: Is Taiwan Already Independent? Dispute With China Explained
content: For decades, the Republic of China in Taipei also claimed to be the legitimate Chinese government, but in 1971 Beijing took over the China seat at the United Nations from Taipei.

title: Kerala CM Announcement LIVE Updates: "Stand By High Command Decision:" KC Venugopal On Kerala Pick
content: Kerala Chief Minister Live Updates: A formal announcement is expected today, once Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge returns to Delhi and holds further talks with senior figures, including Sonia Gandhi.

content: The groom, Devendra Singh Parimar (23), told police that he came in contact with the woman through Instagram.

content: The deceased has been identified as Shekhar. He was a resident of Nalleri village in Devanahalli taluk.

title: China's Door Will Open Wider To US Businesses: Xi Jinping Tells US CEOs
content: Trump-Xi Summit: The US President wants China to stop weaponising supply chains, especially rare earths that power US tech and defence industries.

title: Visionary, Icon, Villain: What China Thinks About Tesla CEO Elon Musk
content: Elon Musk is among a group of more than a dozen CEOs and top executives accompanying US President Donald Trump to Beijing for a summit with Xi Jinping.

content: Since its introduction in 2013, the NEET is now for the first time facing a full-scale cancellation and retest.

content: When asked about the long duration of questioning and the intention behind it, Pawan Khera declined to comment.

title: In Araghchi's India Visit, A #Minab168 Reminder For World
content: The situation surrounding the closure of the Strait of Hormuz-- the vital waterway through which around a fifth of global oil supply passes in peacetime-- is expected to feature prominently in bilateral talks between Araghchi and Jaishankar.

title: Woman Gang-Raped Inside Bus In Delhi, Driver And Conductor Arrested
content: According to the complaint, the woman alleged that two men raped her inside the bus. The bus driver and conductor, have been arrested

title: US Jury Finds 'Chinese Agent' Guilty Of Running 'Secret Police Station'
content: Federal prosecutors in Brooklyn said Lu Jianwang, 64, should have alerted the US Attorney General that he was a Chinese agent.

title: Bengaluru Advocate, 80, Loses Rs 40,000 In Cyber Investment Fraud
content: An 80-year-old Bengaluru advocate lost Rs 40,260 after a deepfake ad tricked him into cyber investment fraud.
